name: Mobile Automation MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing iOS mobile app automation capabilities. Enables AI agents to control iOS simulators and devices, interact with app UI elements, take screenshots, run automated tests, and perform mobile QA workflows through Appium or XCUITest frameworks.
use_cases: Automating iOS app UI testing from mobile QA agents
Controlling iOS simulators for visual regression testing from CI/CD agents
Performing accessibility testing on iOS apps from compliance agents
Building AI-driven mobile app testing workflows for iOS
not_for: Android automation (iOS-specific — use Android ADB MCP for Android)
Teams without macOS and Xcode for iOS simulation
Production device testing without proper device management infrastructure
best_when: A mobile QA engineer or iOS developer wants AI agents to assist with app testing — automating UI interactions, running test scenarios, and capturing screenshots across iOS simulators.
avoid_when: You primarily target Android or cross-platform testing. iOS automation requires macOS with Xcode — not cross-platform.

auth.notes: No authentication — local iOS simulator control via Appium/XCUITest. macOS and Xcode required.

pricing.notes: Free open source. Xcode and Apple Developer account needed for device testing.

known_agent_gotchas: macOS + Xcode + iOS Simulator required — not cross-platform
iOS simulator state can be inconsistent — implement reset between test sessions
UI element identification requires accessibility identifiers — apps must be properly instrumented
Physical device testing requires provisioning profiles and developer certificate
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local iOS automation. macOS only. No external access. App data accessed during testing should be treated as test data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local iOS automation. macOS only. App testing data stays local.
